question: What is the definition of the acronym ECCS?

nuscale_ch01_0188.title: BTP 6-1, (March 2007): pH for Emergency Coolant Water for Pressurized Water ReactorsB.3 Hydrogen Generation from Aluminum Corrosion
nuscale_ch01_0188.summary: The section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding pH control and hydrogen generation from aluminum corrosion in emergency coolant water. While NuScale’s design lacks certain systems referenced in the criteria (such as containment spray systems and sumps), the intent of the guidelines is still met due to the collection and recirculation of ECCS water within the containment vessel. Other criteria related to minimum containment pressure and bypass leakage are deemed not applicable to NuScale’s unique design.

nuscale_ch01_0024.title: Emergency Core Cooling System
nuscale_ch01_0024.summary: The Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS) in the NuScale design is a fully passive system that removes decay heat during a loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA) using three independent vent valves and two recirculation valves, which operate without external power. The system vents steam into containment for condensation and recirculates water to the reactor core, with continued cooling achieved by heat conduction to the reactor pool.

nuscale_ch01_0063.title: Emergency Core Cooling System Valve Design Certification Application Demonstration Test
nuscale_ch01_0063.summary: This section describes the demonstration test program for the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS) valves in the NuScale reactor design. The test program aimed to verify the functional performance of the ECCS valves, including their operation at reactor pressures and temperatures and in borated coolant, as well as the effectiveness of unique design features like the inadvertent actuation block (IAB). Testing was conducted at a specialized facility using a test article representative of the reactor recirculation valve (RRV) to ensure the valves meet safety and operational requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0189.title: BTP 6-2, Rev 3: Minimum Containment Pressure Model for PWR ECCS PerformanceAll (B.1 thru B.3)Various
nuscale_ch01_0189.summary: This section explains that the guidance in BTP 6-2, Rev 3, regarding minimum containment pressure models for PWR ECCS performance, is not applicable to the NuScale design because a postulated LOCA in NuScale does not result in core uncovery. Therefore, the regulatory criteria addressed in this BTP do not apply to NuScale’s containment and ECCS configuration.

edges.description: Section B defines ECCS as the Emergency Core Cooling System, which is referenced in Section A when discussing the collection of ECCS water inside the NuScale containment.

Section A references the operation of the ECCS in the context of water chemistry requirements, while Section B provides specific details about the ECCS valve design and testing, connecting them through the shared focus on ECCS system components and their operational requirements.

Both Section A and Section B discuss aspects of ECCS (Emergency Core Cooling System) operation and its regulatory conformance, referencing the same acceptance criteria regarding water chemistry and containment systems, indicating they are parallel evaluations of ECCS requirements in different regulatory contexts.

Section A defines the acronym ECCS as Emergency Core Cooling System, while Section B uses the acronym ECCS in the context of valve design and testing, thus Section A provides the definition that clarifies the meaning of ECCS as used in Section B.

Section A defines and describes the function and components of the ECCS in the NuScale design, while Section B discusses regulatory criteria and conformance related to ECCS operation, specifically referencing how the NuScale ECCS water collects inside containment in the absence of a spray system or sump.

Both Section A and Section B refer to the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS) in the context of the NuScale reactor design, with Section A describing ECCS valve design and testing, and Section B discussing regulatory criteria and conformance for ECCS performance.
